reasoning: Multiple authoritative climate science organizations, including NOAA, NASA GISS scientists, and Copernicus, have confirmed through the third quarter of 2025 that the year is tracking significantly behind the record set in 2024. As of mid-year, NOAA explicitly estimated the probability of 2025 being the warmest year on record at less than 1%. Copnernicus recently reported in November 2025 that the year is virtually certain to finish as the second- or third-warmest year on record. Given this overwhelming consensus and the insurmountable gap between 2024 and 2025 anomaly values with only two months remaining in the year, the probability is effectively zero, making 0.02 the most appropriate estimate.
